title: The Sapphire of Shadows
subtitle: A Murder Mystery Party for 13 Players
crime: The Sapphire of Shadows is stolen. Dr. Priya Mehta is found unconscious.
story: Tonight was supposed to be the most glamorous event of the year. The Ravenswood Gala was organized to celebrate the public unveiling of a priceless artifact: the Sapphire of Shadows, a rare gemstone discovered by Dr. Priya Mehta after three years of fieldwork in Eastern Europe.

The stone carries a reputation -- some call it cursed, others call it the most valuable privately held artifact in the world. Either way, tonight was its debut. Everyone here received a personal invitation.
The Sapphire sat in a locked display case at the center of the room. Most guests admired it once and moved on. One person kept coming back.

At exactly 8:47 PM, the lights in the display alcove flickered. Within minutes, the Sapphire was gone and Dr. Priya Mehta was found unconscious on the floor of the back storage room. Nobody heard anything. Nobody saw anything. And everyone claims they have no idea what happened.
readAloud: Welcome to the Ravenswood Gala. Tonight we were celebrating the unveiling of a priceless gemstone -- the Sapphire of Shadows. But something has gone very wrong. The Sapphire has been stolen and Dr. Priya Mehta, its discoverer, has been found unconscious in the back storage room. The police are on their way. Nobody leaves this room.

Everyone here had a connection to this gala. Someone planned this. Someone executed it. And someone is going to get caught tonight. You have ten minutes to read your character card privately. Then the investigation begins.

rules.title: Play Your Character
rules.text: Your card tells you who you are at this gala, what you observed, and where you were at 8:47 PM.

rules.title: You May Lie
rules.text: You can lie about anything except facts on your card that are cross-confirmed by another player's card.

rules.title: Talk To Three Specific People
rules.text: Your card lists 3 people you MUST have a real conversation with. You choose what to say.

rules.title: Bring Clues Quietly to the Hosts
rules.text: Clues are hidden around the room. If you find one, do NOT announce it. Bring it privately to Siddhi or Mohit. They log it and reveal it at the next meeting only.

rules.title: Clues Only Appear If Found
rules.text: If a clue is never found, it never enters the game. Searching matters.

rules.title: Three Meetings
rules.text: At each meeting the hosts reveal every clue found that phase, read them aloud, and name who found them. Then the next phase begins.

rules.title: Final Written Accusation
rules.text: At the end everyone writes: (1) who stole the Sapphire, (2) why, (3) what evidence supports it. Majority wins.

timeline.details: Players read character cards and begin talking. Required conversations happen. Players search for clues and bring them quietly to the hosts.
Hosts reveal ONLY Pack A clues that were actually found. Each clue read aloud, finder named. Brief open discussion.
Re-interrogation using new information. More clue searching. Found clues brought quietly to hosts.
Hosts reveal ONLY Pack B clues that were found. Same format.
Final interrogations. Last clue search. Players lock in their theories.
Hosts reveal ONLY Pack C clues that were found. Everyone writes their final accusation. Hosts tally and reveal the truth.
